segments.caption: The video opens with a stunning view of Earth from space, showcasing its continents and oceans against a starry backdrop, with the PBS logo in the top left corner. The scene transitions to a woman in a blue long-sleeve shirt and gray leggings standing against a dark, textured background, gesturing as she speaks. The setting shifts to an underwater scene with beams of light penetrating deep blue water, followed by a stormy beach with waves crashing onto a pebble shore. A close-up of green, chain-like microorganisms appears, suggesting early life forms. The video then displays a split Earth graphic, half showing the planet’s surface and half revealing its fiery interior. The woman reappears, continuing her explanation. A graph titled 'Average Temperatures During the Mesoproterozoic' is shown, with red and blue lines depicting temperature variations by latitude, and a red circle marking the tropics' average temperature. The scene transitions to a bright sun illuminating Earth from space, followed by a detailed tectonic plate map of Earth’s surface, color-coded to show plate boundaries and movements. Finally, a cross-section of Earth’s interior is displayed, labeled with 'Crust,' 'Upper Mantle,' and 'Lower Mantle,' illustrating the planet’s layered structure.

segments.caption: The video begins with a stunning view of Earth from space, showcasing its continents and oceans against a starry backdrop, with the PBS logo in the top left corner. This transitions to a timeline graphic illustrating Earth’s geological eons—Hadean, Archean, Proterozoic, and Phanerozoic—highlighting the 'Boring Billion' period in dark gray. The scene shifts back to Earth from space, now illuminated by the sun, before moving to a cross-section diagram of Earth’s interior, depicting tectonic plates and mantle convection. A close-up of green, chain-like microorganisms follows, suggesting early life forms. The video then shows an aerial view of the Dingvellir Rift Valley, with text labeling the location, followed by animated diagrams of tectonic plate movements, including subduction and rifting, credited to DOMDOMEGG. A global tectonic plate map appears, with plates outlined in orange and specific regions highlighted in yellow and green. The Woman in blue shirt then appears, standing against a dark textured background, gesturing as she speaks. The final scene is a serene, animated coastal environment with yellow microbial mats in shallow water, surrounded by rocky outcrops and distant mountains under a hazy sky, evoking early Earth conditions.

segments.caption: The video begins with an animated coastal scene depicting shallow, calm waters with yellow microbial mats floating on the surface, surrounded by rocky outcrops under a hazy, star-dusted sky—suggesting early Earth conditions. The scene transitions to a woman in a blue long-sleeve shirt and gray leggings standing against a dark textured background, gesturing as she speaks. She appears twice in the segment, maintaining consistent attire and demeanor. As she explains, a 3D animated diagram of tectonic plates appears beside her, illustrating subduction and mantle convection with arrows showing movement. This is followed by a cross-sectional diagram labeled 'MODERN MANTLE CONVECTION,' showing red and blue arrows indicating heat-driven circulation within Earth’s mantle. The woman reappears, now with an inset image of a rock sample, possibly a banded-iron formation, which is later shown in a close-up with the label 'BANDED-IRON FORMATION' and image credit to James St. John. A world map then appears, highlighting regions in orange to indicate the 'AGE OF LAST THERMO-TECTONIC EVENT,' with a legend specifying geological time periods including the Archean. The video returns to the woman speaking, then revisits the 'MODERN MANTLE CONVECTION' diagram before showing a close-up of a weathered Precambrian lava surface, credited to Black Tusk. The segment concludes with the animated coastal scene reappearing, reinforcing the early Earth environment.

segments.caption: The video begins with a stylized cross-section of Earth’s interior, showing a mountain range atop layered strata, with a red dot descending through the layers, symbolizing depth. This transitions to a horizontal timeline graphic labeled with geological eons: Hadean, Archean, 'Boring Billion', and Phanerozoic, spanning from 4.6 BYA to Present Day. The scene shifts to a detailed cross-section of Earth’s layers, with labeled depths: -100 km (60 mi), -700 km (430 mi), and 2900 km (1800 mi), identifying the Lithosphere. This is followed by real-world images of weathered Precambrian lava and banded-iron formations, emphasizing ancient rock structures. A woman in a blue shirt appears, standing against a dark textured background, gesturing as she speaks, likely explaining geological concepts. The video then displays a split Earth graphic, with one half showing the surface and the other revealing a fiery interior, followed by a return to the timeline. Next, a labeled Earth’s interior cross-section appears, identifying the Crust, Upper Mantle, and Lower Mantle. The segment concludes with an animated coastal scene depicting shallow water with yellow microbial mats, rocky shores, and distant mountains under a hazy sky, suggesting early Earth conditions.

segments.caption: The video begins with a serene coastal scene, showing a pebble-covered beach under a stormy sky with waves crashing in, setting a dramatic tone. This transitions to a diagram labeled 'ANCIENT MANTLE CONVECTION,' illustrating Earth’s interior with red and blue arrows depicting convection currents, overlaid on a semi-circular cross-section of the mantle. The scene then shifts to the Woman in blue shirt, who stands against a dark textured background, gesturing as she explains the concept, with the mantle convection diagram appearing in a small box beside her. Next, a detailed tectonic plate map is shown, highlighting plate boundaries with yellow and red lines, followed by a view of Earth from space labeled 'NUNA,' depicting a supercontinent with visible landmasses and oceans. A timeline graphic then appears, marking geological eons from 4.6 BYA to present day, with a red line indicating the formation of the Nuna supercontinent during the Proterozoic. The video transitions to a stormy beach again, then to a deep blue underwater scene with light rays penetrating from above, creating a mysterious ambiance. Finally, chemical symbols appear on screen: 'O₂ ↓' and 'H₂S ↑', followed by the word 'EUXINIA,' indicating a shift in ocean chemistry with decreasing oxygen and increasing hydrogen sulfide, suggesting a period of widespread anoxic and sulfidic oceans.

segments.caption: The video begins with a close-up animation of a single cell, showcasing a translucent blue sphere with a green nucleus, surrounded by other similar cells in a blue, ethereal environment, suggesting a microscopic biological setting. The scene transitions to a woman in a blue long-sleeve shirt and gray leggings standing against a dark textured background, gesturing as she speaks. To her right, an inset image appears with the label 'PROKARYOTES', showing stylized green bacteria with flagella. The video then cuts to a detailed illustration of various bacterial forms in green and brown hues, including rods, spheres, and spirals, emphasizing microbial diversity. This is followed by a close-up of green, chain-like microorganisms, possibly cyanobacteria, moving gently in a light-filled environment. The scene shifts to a real-world example: a pinkish-red body of water labeled 'PURPLE SULFUR BACTERIA', indicating a natural habitat for these organisms. Next, a laboratory jar is shown with layered, colorful microbial growth, including red and green bands, illustrating a controlled environment for studying such bacteria. The woman reappears, continuing her explanation with expressive hand gestures. The video then transitions to a realistic depiction of Earth from space, showing continents and oceans against a starry backdrop, before cutting to a microscopic view of various microorganisms, including teardrop-shaped and circular forms, floating in a watery medium, highlighting the diversity of early life forms.

segments.caption: The video begins with a stylized world map in sepia tones, transitioning to a close-up of a microscopic view showing a cluster of microorganisms with orange and green hues, surrounded by bubbles, suggesting early life forms. This shifts to a serene animated coastal scene with shallow water and yellow microbial mats, evoking early Earth conditions. The scene then cuts to a woman in a blue long-sleeve shirt and gray leggings standing against a dark textured background, gesturing as she speaks. She reappears later with a diagram of Earth’s interior layers to her right, illustrating tectonic processes. The video then presents a realistic Earth from space, showing a supercontinent labeled 'RODINIA' with visible ice caps and oceans. This is followed by a diagram labeled 'ANCIENT MANTLE CONVECTION,' depicting convection currents with red and blue arrows. The Earth from space reappears, now with a more detailed view of Rodinia, emphasizing its vast landmass and surrounding seas. The woman returns, continuing her explanation, and the video concludes with a zoomed-in view of Rodinia, highlighting its central ice cap and surrounding landforms.

segments.caption: The video begins with a satellite view of Earth, focusing on a large landmass surrounded by oceans, with visible cloud cover and a starry space background, establishing a global perspective. The scene transitions to a stylized cross-section of Earth’s interior, showing layered structures from crust to core, with a mountain range at the top. This evolves into a diagram labeled 'ANCIENT MANTLE CONVECTION,' illustrating convection currents with red and blue arrows moving through the mantle. The scene then cuts to a woman in a blue long-sleeve shirt and gray leggings standing against a dark textured background, gesturing as she speaks, with the 'ANCIENT MANTLE CONVECTION' diagram displayed in a box to her right. She continues speaking, and the diagram disappears, leaving her alone in the frame. The video returns to the satellite view of Earth, then transitions to a 3D cross-sectional animation of a tectonic rift, showing two landmasses moving apart with an upwelling of magma beneath a body of water, indicated by white arrows and a glowing orange core. The animation emphasizes the geological process of rifting and mantle upwelling, visually connecting the earlier mantle convection concept to surface tectonics.

segments.caption: The video begins with a colorful tectonic plate map showing Earth’s surface with vibrant red, yellow, green, and blue zones indicating geological activity, particularly along mid-ocean ridges. This transitions to an underwater scene where white plumes of hydrothermal fluid rise from the seafloor, suggesting active venting. The scene then cuts to a woman in a blue long-sleeve shirt and gray leggings standing against a dark textured background, gesturing as she speaks, with a small inset of the underwater venting scene in the top right corner. The video then shifts to a series of animated paleogeographic maps showing Earth’s continents from 166 Ma (Jurassic) to 65 Ma (Paleocene), illustrating the gradual separation of Pangea into modern continents, with labels indicating geological periods and a citation to Scotese, C.R., 2019. Following this, a stormy beach with crashing waves and dark skies is shown, transitioning to a view of Earth from space, partially illuminated by the sun, with a visible crack across its surface. The final scene is an aerial view of the Dingvellir Rift Valley, a rugged landscape with a river cutting through rocky terrain, labeled 'DINGVELLIR RIFT VALLEY' in white text.

discovered_new_entities.Animated paleogeographic maps: A sequence of animated world maps showing Earth’s continents from 166 Ma (Jurassic) to 65 Ma (Paleocene), illustrating the breakup of Pangea, with labels for geological periods and a citation to Scotese, C.R., 2019.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of green, chain-like microorganisms, possibly cyanobacteria, floating in a bright, watery environment, suggesting early life forms. The scene transitions to a stunning view of Earth from space, rotating slowly against a starry backdrop, highlighting its blue oceans and green continents. This shifts to a dramatic prehistoric scene with dinosaurs in a misty, forested landscape, overlaid with the bold white text 'EONS' and the PBS logo. The narrative then moves to a woman in a blue shirt driving a car, with a custom license plate reading 'PALEO ROX' and a Texas state emblem, indicating a passion for paleontology. The video cuts to an aerial shot of a dirt road cutting through a green, rocky landscape, with the text 'PREHISTORIC ROAD TRIP' appearing prominently. The woman is then seen outdoors in a grassy field, holding a fossil, while another person works nearby with a screen. Inside a museum or lab, she stands beside a man writing on a chalkboard labeled 'Mammal relatives' and 'Modern Reptile relatives,' discussing evolutionary biology. The scene returns to an aerial view of a rugged, cracked rock formation with a car driving through, overlaid with the text 'TWO BILLION YEARS.' The video concludes with a promotional graphic for 'PREHISTORIC ROAD TRIP,' listing broadcast dates and streaming availability, followed by a deep blue underwater scene with fish swimming, and a thank-you message to contributors, including 'LUCAN CURTIS-MAHONEY, SEAN DENNIS, JAKE HART, JON DAVISON NG, PATRICK SEIFERT, AND STEVE!'

discovered_new_entities.Underwater scene with fish: A deep blue underwater environment with fish swimming, including a striped fish and a shark, with light rays penetrating the water, creating a serene and mysterious atmosphere.
discovered_new_entities.Thank-you message: White text on a dark blue underwater background reading 'THANKS TO THIS MONTH'S EONTOLOGISTS: LUCAN CURTIS-MAHONEY, SEAN DENNIS, JAKE HART, JON DAVISON NG, PATRICK SEIFERT, AND STEVE!'

segments.caption: The video segment begins with an underwater scene featuring a deep blue aquatic environment with fish, including a striped fish and a shark, swimming gracefully as light rays penetrate the water, creating a serene atmosphere. Overlaid on this scene is a thank-you message in white text acknowledging 'THIS MONTH'S EONTOLOGISTS' by name. The scene then transitions to a woman in a blue long-sleeve shirt and gray leggings standing against a dark textured background, gesturing expressively as she speaks. On-screen text appears, displaying 'PATREON.COM/EONS' in large white letters, followed by a green box stating 'THIS EPISODE WAS FILMED IN THE KONSTANTIN HAASE STUDIO!' as she continues to gesture. The video then cuts back to the underwater scene, now displaying detailed end credits for the show 'Eons' by PBS Digital Studios, listing production roles, music, image credits, and patrons. The segment concludes with a solid blue screen featuring the white PBS logo centered prominently.

active_entities.Underwater scene: A deep blue underwater environment with fish swimming, including a striped fish and a shark, with light rays penetrating the water, creating a serene and mysterious atmosphere.
active_entities.Woman in blue shirt: A woman wearing a blue long-sleeve shirt and gray leggings, standing against a dark textured background, gesturing while speaking. She appears twice in the segment, maintaining consistent attire and demeanor.
active_entities.Thank-you message: White text on a dark blue underwater background reading 'THANKS TO THIS MONTH'S EONTOLOGISTS: LUCAN CURTIS-MAHONEY, SEAN DENNIS, JAKE HART, JON DAVISON NG, PATRICK SEIFERT, AND STEVE!'

discovered_new_entities.Patreon text overlay: Large white text reading 'PATREON.COM/EONS' displayed on the left side of the screen during the woman's segment.
discovered_new_entities.Studio credit box: A green rectangular box with white text stating 'THIS EPISODE WAS FILMED IN THE KONSTANTIN HAASE STUDIO!' appearing on the right side of the screen during the woman's segment.
discovered_new_entities.End credits screen: A detailed credit list for 'Eons' by PBS Digital Studios, including production roles, music, image sources, and patron names, overlaid on the underwater scene.
discovered_new_entities.PBS logo: The white PBS logo, featuring a stylized head silhouette inside a circle, centered on a solid blue background at the end of the segment.

entity_library.Woman in blue shirt: A woman wearing a blue long-sleeve shirt and gray leggings, standing against a dark textured background, gesturing while speaking. She appears twice in the segment, maintaining consistent attire and demeanor.
entity_library.Earth from space: A realistic depiction of Earth with visible continents and oceans, shown against a starry background, rotating slowly and later illuminated by the sun with a visible crack across its surface.
entity_library.Underwater scene: A deep blue underwater environment with fish swimming, including a striped fish and a shark, with light rays penetrating the water, creating a serene and mysterious atmosphere.
entity_library.Stormy beach: A pebble-covered shoreline with waves crashing under a cloudy, stormy sky, suggesting a dynamic coastal environment.
entity_library.Microorganisms: Green, chain-like structures resembling early life forms, possibly cyanobacteria, shown in a close-up view, floating in a bright, watery environment.
entity_library.Split Earth graphic: A stylized Earth divided vertically, with one half showing the surface and the other half revealing a glowing, fiery interior, symbolizing Earth's internal structure.
entity_library.Temperature graph: A line graph titled 'Average Temperatures During the Mesoproterozoic,' with red and blue lines indicating temperature changes by latitude, and a red circle marking the tropics' average temperature.
entity_library.Tectonic plate map: A colorful, detailed map of Earth’s surface showing major tectonic plates with labeled boundaries and movements, using a heat-map style to indicate geological activity, particularly along mid-ocean ridges.
entity_library.Earth's interior cross-section: A diagram of Earth’s layers, including the crust, upper mantle, and lower mantle, with labels pointing to each layer.
entity_library.Timeline graphic: A horizontal bar chart showing Earth’s geological eons from 4.6 BYA to present day, with the 'Boring Billion' period highlighted in dark gray.
entity_library.Dingvellir Rift Valley aerial view: An aerial shot of a rugged, rocky valley with a river running through it, labeled 'DINGVELLIR RIFT VALLEY' in white text, showcasing geological features.
entity_library.Animated tectonic diagrams: Simplified, colorful illustrations of tectonic plate interactions, including subduction and rifting, with arrows indicating movement, credited to DOMDOMEGG.
entity_library.Animated coastal scene: A serene, computer-generated landscape depicting shallow water with yellow microbial mats, rocky shores, and distant mountains under a hazy sky, suggesting early Earth conditions.
entity_library.Banded-iron formation: A close-up of a rock sample with distinct gray and black layered bands, labeled 'BANDED-IRON FORMATION' with image credit to James St. John.
entity_library.Weathered surface of Precambrian lava: A close-up of a rough, cracked rock surface with a grayish texture, labeled 'WEATHERED SURFACE OF PRECAMBRIAN LAVA' with image credit to Black Tusk.
entity_library.MODERN MANTLE CONVECTION diagram: A semi-circular cross-section of Earth’s mantle with red and blue arrows illustrating convection currents, labeled 'MODERN MANTLE CONVECTION'.
entity_library.World map of thermo-tectonic events: A grayscale world map with orange regions indicating the 'AGE OF LAST THERMO-TECTONIC EVENT,' including a legend for geological time periods such as Archean, Early Proterozoic, etc.
entity_library.ANCIENT MANTLE CONVECTION diagram: A semi-circular cross-section of Earth’s mantle with red and blue arrows illustrating convection currents, labeled 'ANCIENT MANTLE CONVECTION'.
entity_library.NUNA Earth model: A stylized Earth model showing a supercontinent named 'NUNA' with visible landmasses and oceans, set against a starry background.
entity_library.Chemical symbols overlay: On-screen text displaying 'O₂ ↓' and 'H₂S ↑' with arrows, followed by the word 'EUXINIA', indicating changes in ocean chemistry.
entity_library.Purple sulfur bacteria: A pinkish-red body of water with floating debris, labeled 'PURPLE SULFUR BACTERIA', indicating a natural habitat for these organisms.
entity_library.Laboratory jar with microbial layers: A transparent jar containing layered, colorful microbial growth, including red and green bands, illustrating a controlled environment for studying bacteria.
entity_library.Illustration of diverse bacteria: A detailed, artistic rendering of various bacterial shapes in green and brown hues, including rods, spheres, spirals, and chains, set against a light background, emphasizing microbial diversity.
entity_library.Microscopic view of mixed microorganisms: A close-up, real-time microscopic image showing various microorganisms, including teardrop-shaped and circular forms, floating in a watery medium, with some exhibiting internal structures and colors.
entity_library.RODINIA Earth model: A stylized Earth model showing a supercontinent named 'RODINIA' with visible landmasses and oceans, set against a starry background, featuring a central ice cap and surrounding seas.
entity_library.3D tectonic rift animation: A computer-generated 3D cross-section showing two landmasses moving apart with a body of water above, and a glowing orange magma upwelling beneath, with white arrows indicating plate movement.
entity_library.Animated paleogeographic maps: A sequence of animated world maps showing Earth’s continents from 166 Ma (Jurassic) to 65 Ma (Paleocene), illustrating the breakup of Pangea, with labels for geological periods and a citation to Scotese, C.R., 2019.
entity_library.Prehistoric scene with dinosaurs: A misty, forested landscape with dinosaurs, including a large theropod and a smaller, spiky dinosaur, overlaid with the bold white text 'EONS' and the PBS logo.
entity_library.Custom license plate: A Texas license plate with the text 'PALEO ROX' in blue letters, featuring a dinosaur silhouette and the state's 'Lone Star' emblem, mounted on a car's rear bumper.
entity_library.Aerial dirt road view: An aerial shot of a narrow dirt road cutting through a green, rocky landscape, with the text 'PREHISTORIC ROAD TRIP' superimposed in large white letters.
entity_library.Fossil excavation site: An outdoor scene in a grassy field where a woman in a blue shirt holds a fossil, while another person works with a screen, suggesting paleontological fieldwork.
entity_library.Chalkboard discussion: A man writing on a chalkboard labeled 'Mammal relatives' and 'Modern Reptile relatives' while a woman in a blue shirt observes, set in a museum or lab environment with fossil shelves in the background.
entity_library.Rugged rock formation with car: An aerial view of a cracked, rugged rock formation with a car driving through, overlaid with the text 'TWO BILLION YEARS' in bold white and black letters.
entity_library.Promotional graphic: A dark green background with a framed image of a dirt road through greenery, displaying 'PREHISTORIC ROAD TRIP' in large white letters, along with broadcast dates and streaming information.
entity_library.Underwater scene with fish: A deep blue underwater environment with fish swimming, including a striped fish and a shark, with light rays penetrating the water, creating a serene and mysterious atmosphere.
entity_library.Thank-you message: White text on a dark blue underwater background reading 'THANKS TO THIS MONTH'S EONTOLOGISTS: LUCAN CURTIS-MAHONEY, SEAN DENNIS, JAKE HART, JON DAVISON NG, PATRICK SEIFERT, AND STEVE!'
entity_library.Patreon text overlay: Large white text reading 'PATREON.COM/EONS' displayed on the left side of the screen during the woman's segment.
entity_library.Studio credit box: A green rectangular box with white text stating 'THIS EPISODE WAS FILMED IN THE KONSTANTIN HAASE STUDIO!' appearing on the right side of the screen during the woman's segment.
entity_library.End credits screen: A detailed credit list for 'Eons' by PBS Digital Studios, including production roles, music, image sources, and patron names, overlaid on the underwater scene.
entity_library.PBS logo: The white PBS logo, featuring a stylized head silhouette inside a circle, centered on a solid blue background at the end of the segment.
